column1: # Simon the Sorcerer 繁體中文譯表
# 格式: 英文<TAB>繁體中文
# 由 tools/build_translation.py 編成 Big5 的 simon_zh.tab
4 gold pieces! That's a quality gemstone that is.
column2: 四枚金幣！這可是塊上等寶石啊。
一把輕量碳化鎢羊角錘。

column1: A beekeeper's hat and fashion accessory.
column2: 一頂兼具時尚配件功能的養蜂帽。

column1: A box of groceries for the Goblins.
A cake covered in pink icing.
column2: 一個覆著粉紅糖霜的蛋糕。

column1: A collection of hot, metal spikes.
A few sit-ups would do you the world of good.
column2: 做幾下仰臥起坐對你大有裨益。

column1: A fine example of a bucket of water.
A fine example of it's kind.
A fine example of its kind.
A fire alarm bell with a push button center.
column2: 一個中央有按鈕的火警警鈴。

column1: A garden tap which is caked with rust.
column2: 一個鏽跡斑斑的庭院水龍頭。

column1: A goblin key made from two teeth and a pencil.
column2: 一把用兩顆牙齒和一支鉛筆做的哥布林鑰匙。

column1: A heap of highly magical - and smelly - compost.
column2: 一堆魔力高強——而且臭氣熏天的堆肥。

column1: A hole in the ceiling to let light in.
column2: 天花板上用來採光的洞。

column1: A hook attached to a length of rope.
A hook used for hanging a pot over the fire.
column2: 一個用來在火上掛鍋的鉤子。

column1: A huge pile of sparkling gems.
column2: 一大堆閃閃發光的寶石。
一個看起來很開朗的酒保。

column1: A large Goblin guard is blocking the passage.
column2: 一個高大的哥布林守衛擋住了通道。

column1: A long plait of blonde hair tied on securely.
column2: 一條牢牢繫住的金色長辮子。

column1: A lump of metal bearing rock.
A metal bar from inside a bell.
column2: 一根從鐘裡取出的金屬棒。

column1: A metal slops bucket. There is a hole in the side of it.
column2: 一個金屬餿水桶。側面有個洞。

column1: A piece of wood with a burnt end.
A pile of dirt excavated by Dr Von Jones.
column2: Von Jones 博士挖出的一堆泥土。

column1: A pile of dust and bandages.
A pile of empty beer bottles.
A pin used as a foothold for climbing across a rockface.
column2: 一根用來攀爬岩壁當腳踏的岩釘。
一根看起來威力強大的巫師法杖。

column1: A roll of Super XXXXX Strong mints.
column2: 一捲超強超強超強超強超強薄荷糖。

column1: A set of ladders leading down to the workface.
column2: 一副通往工作面的梯子。

column1: A set of ladders leading out the back of the Swampling's house.
column2: 一副通往 Swampling 家後方的梯子。

column1: A shallow drawer in the Wizard's writing desk.
column2: 巫師書桌裡的一個淺抽屜。

column1: A shifty looking bloke selling bric-a-brac.
column2: 一個鬼鬼祟祟賣舊貨的傢伙。

column1: A shiny lever just waiting to be moved.
column2: 一根閃亮、正等著被拉動的操縱桿。

column1: A single strand of white dog hair.
A small crack in the door.
A small hook to hang things on.
column2: 一個用來掛東西的小鉤子。

column1: A small mouse hole in the skirting board.
column2: 踢腳板裡的一個小老鼠洞。

column1: A small squeaky thing that likes cheese.
column2: 一個吱吱叫又愛吃起司的小東西。

column1: A smelly liquid in a jar.
A solid stone statue of a wizard.
column2: 一尊堅固的石製巫師雕像。

column1: A special herb with strange properties.
column2: 一種有奇特功效的藥草。
一個特別堅硬的斧頭頭。

column1: A specimen collecting jar for invertebrates.
column2: 一個無脊椎動物採集罐。

column1: A splodge of pink spray paint.
A statue of a pygmy warrior woman.
column2: 一尊矮人女戰士的雕像。

column1: A statue of a really ugly barbarian.
column2: 一座醜到不行的野蠻人雕像。

column1: A stone table used for the sacrificing of shaved lions.
column2: 一張用來獻祭剃毛獅子的石桌。

column1: A strange picture with strange symbols around it.
column2: 一幅周圍環繞奇怪符號的奇怪圖畫。

column1: A temporary miscalculation on your part.
A trapdoor set in the floor.
A very poor specimen. It hasn't even got any ears.
column2: 一個很差的標本。連耳朵都沒有。

column1: A voucher for a free glass of beer at the Drunken Druid.
column2: 一張可在醉德魯伊酒館兌換免費啤酒的禮券。
啊哈！意料之中的附帶細則。

column1: Actually, I'm having second thoughts about the whole thing.
column2: 其實，我對整件事開始有點猶豫了。

column1: Actually, it's me. Y'know - the prince of darkness.
column2: 其實是我。你知道的——黑暗王子。

column1: And a fine bridge it is too.
And just where do you two fit into these plans?
column2: 那你們倆在這計畫裡算什麼？

column1: And then I get made a wizard, right?
column2: 然後我就會成為巫師了，對吧？

column1: And what consequences would those be?
And what makes you think I'm not a wizard, eh?
column2: 那你憑什麼覺得我不是巫師，嗯？

column1: And you reckon you're gonna find it down that hole?
column2: 你以為你能在那個洞裡找到它？

column1: Any idea where I can get a human skull?
column2: 知道我哪裡能弄到一顆人頭骨嗎？

column1: Any idea where it might be?
Apparently its some sort of magical metal polish.
column2: 顯然是某種魔法金屬拋光劑。

column1: Apparently the place is called the Drunken Druid.
column2: 顯然那個地方叫「醉鬼德魯伊」。
你是隻聰明的貓頭鷹嗎？

column1: Are you interested in any double glazing?
column2: 您對雙層玻璃窗有興趣嗎？

column1: Are you some sort of Bogieperson?
Are you some sort of sewage worker?
column2: 你是某種下水道工人嗎？

column1: Are you trying to build yourself up again or something?
column2: 你是想重新壯大自己還是怎樣？

column1: As a matter of fact I haven't.

column2: 嗚嗚。可憐的小惡魔寶寶們。

column1: Awwww. Don't cry. I've come to see you.
Back in a bit. Just got to sort a few details.
column2: 馬上回來。先處理幾個細節。

column1: Be gone, demon! Tell me how to use it or I send you back whence you cameth!
column2: 滾吧，惡魔！告訴我怎麼用它，否則我就把你打回你來的地方！

column1: Beware, evil one, or face my wrath!
column2: 當心點，邪惡之徒，否則嘗嘗我的怒火！

column1: Bleach, detergent, washing powder and window cleaner.
column2: 漂白水、洗衣精、洗衣粉和玻璃清潔劑。

column1: Blibble Blibble, Fish Wobble. Kerplunk Rivet Mango.
column2: 布利布利，魚兒搖搖。咕咚鉚釘芒果。

column1: Brochures for the Fiery Pits of Rondor.
But I'm standing in for Axeface Skullsplitter.
column2: 但我是在替 Axeface Skullsplitter 頂班的。
但是，我可是個強大的巫師。

column1: By the sacred beard of J.R.R.Tolkien, prepare to DIE!!!
column2: 以 J.R.R.Tolkien 神聖的鬍鬚起誓，受死吧！！！

column1: Call me stupid, but I don't find that method completely fair.
column2: 就算我笨吧，但我覺得這方法不怎麼公平。

column1: Calypso told me to look for four wizards disguised as country bumpkins.
column2: Calypso 叫我找四個偽裝成鄉巴佬的巫師。

column1: Can I have a last request?
column2: 我能有個最後的請求嗎？

column1: Can I have your er... thingy?
column2: 可以給我那個……那個什麼東西嗎？
可以讓我看點別的東西嗎？

column1: Can you do me a Cool Red Witch?
Can't you eat something other than goat?
column2: 你就不能吃點羊以外的東西嗎？

column1: Cease your fishing. I am a purveyor of finest Stew de Swamp.
column2: 別釣魚了。我可是頂級「沼澤燉菜」的供應商。

column1: Cower, demon! I'm a powerful wizard.
column2: 發抖吧，惡魔！我可是個強大的巫師。

column1: D'ya think he's around these parts?
column2: 你覺得他會在這附近嗎？

column1: D'ya wanna make something of it?
D'you know anything about the evil wizard, Sordid?
column2: 你知道那個邪惡巫師 Sordid 的事嗎？

column1: D'you know where I can find the key to it?
column2: 你知道我哪裡能找到它的鑰匙嗎？

column1: Damn. I've forgotten what they were now.
column2: 該死。我現在忘了它們是什麼了。

column1: Deary me. Who forgot their sunscreen then?
column2: 哎呀呀。是誰忘了擦防曬啊？

column1: Did he think up the fishing rod, as well?
Do I get a Young Wizards check book and pen?
column2: 我能拿到一本「青年巫師」支票簿和筆嗎？

column1: Do you do special rates for mighty wizards?
column2: 你們對強大巫師有特價嗎？

column1: Do you know anything about this 'Sordid' geezer?
column2: 你知道這個叫 Sordid 的老傢伙的事嗎？

column1: Do you know where Calypso has got to?
column2: 你知道 Calypso 去哪了嗎？

column1: Do you think there may be some around here?
Don't make me laugh. I want at least 40.
column2: 別搞笑了。我至少要 40 個。

column1: Don't really see as there's any alternative.
column2: 實在看不出有什麼別的辦法。

column1: Don't waste it. I quite fancy a bowl now.
column2: 別浪費。我現在還挺想吃一碗的。

column1: Don't you call me precious, mate.
Don't you get a little cold during the winter?
Dr Von Jones searching for fossils.
column2: Von Jones 博士在尋找化石。

column1: Enough beer for a really good night.
column2: 足夠讓今晚喝得盡興的啤酒。

column1: Er... Have you heard the one about the vicar and the mongoose.
column2: 呃... 你聽過那個關於牧師和貓鼬的笑話嗎。

column1: Er... I was hoping you might know.
column2: 呃... 我本來希望你可能知道。

column1: Er... Sorry I left yours at home.
column2: 呃... 抱歉我把你的留在家裡了。

column1: Excuse me but you've spelt 'rites' wrong on your placard.
column2: 打擾一下,但你牌子上的 'rites' 拼錯了。

column1: Excuse me, sir. Do you have a license to fish here?
column2: 打擾一下,先生。你在這裡釣魚有許可證嗎?

column1: Excuse me. I'm off to talk to some saner people.
column2: 失陪了。我要去找一些比較正常的人說話。

column1: Excuse me. I've got a train to catch.

column2: 極沉重的青銅鍍層大門。

column1: Fear not, sweet virgin, I SHALL save you!
column2: 別怕,純潔的少女,我「一定」會救妳!

column1: Fighting is totally against my principles.
column2: 打架完全違背我的原則。

column1: Fine, I'll find another way across then.
column2: 好吧,那我自己找別的路過去。

column1: Fine, I'll go get some goat for you, OK?
column2: 好吧,我去幫你弄點山羊肉來,行吧?

column1: Fine, I'll just go and get some.
Fine. Anything to help a woodworm in distress.
column2: 好吧。幫助遇難的蛀蟲嘛,義不容辭。

column1: Fine. I know when I'm not wanted.
column2: 好吧。我知道我什麼時候不受歡迎。

column1: Fine. When the world ends, on your head be it.
column2: 好吧。世界末日時,後果自負。

column1: Forgive me, fair one, I'm not worthy to stand here.
column2: 原諒我,美人,我不配站在這裡。

column1: Geeze, what's the world coming to, eh?
column2: 天哪,這世界變成什麼樣了,嗯?

column1: Give me the metal detector and I'll have a look around.
column2: 把金屬探測器給我,我來四處看看。

column1: Go on then, what's my future?
column2: 那就說吧,我的未來如何?
你知道的,山羊不會說話。

column1: Ha, foolish creatures. I'm traveling in disguise.
column2: 哈,愚蠢的生物。我正在喬裝旅行。

column1: Hang on... Did you say PIGLET?
Happy birthday. I've got you a new axe head!
column2: 生日快樂。我給你弄了個新斧頭!

column1: Have you always wanted to be barbarians?
column2: 你們一直都想當野蠻人嗎?

column1: Have you got a permit for this, sir?
column2: 先生,你有這個的許可證嗎?

column1: Have you got any dodgy salesman repellent?
column2: 你有那種驅逐可疑推銷員的噴霧嗎?

column1: Have you got any hint books for this game?
column2: 你有這遊戲的攻略本嗎?

column1: Have you got any hints for me?
Have you remembered what it was you wanted yet?
column2: 你想起你想要什麼了嗎?

column1: He appears to be in charge.
He looks completely out of it.
column2: 他看起來完全神志不清。

column1: He must have turned Calypso into one!
column2: 他一定把 Calypso 也變成了一個!

column1: He seems to be in a drunken stupor.
He was caught short I'm afraid.
column2: 恐怕他一時內急憋不住了。

column1: He's assaying the gems from the mine.
column2: 他正在檢驗礦坑裡的寶石。

column1: He's crying because he's got a thorn stuck in the bottom of his foot.
column2: 他在哭,因為腳底紮了一根刺。

column1: He's got a bucket over his head.
He's guarding a huge pile of gems.
He's making an awful noise with his sousaphone.
column2: 他用蘇沙號製造出可怕的噪音。

column1: He's polymorphed back into a frog.
He's resting after the shock of having the thorn pulled out of his foot.
column2: 拔出腳底的刺後驚魂未定,他正在休息。

column1: He's stopping anyone crossing the bridge.
He's trying to cast a spell to kill me!
He's trying to relight the pit.
column2: 他正試圖重新點燃坑洞。

column1: He's using a drill to mine further into the rock.
column2: 他用鑽子往岩石裡挖得更深。

column1: He's waiting for me to repair his sousaphone.
column2: 他在等我幫他修好蘇沙號。
最近聽到什麼有趣的八卦嗎。

column1: Hi. I'm doing a survey for Warrior Weekly. Would you care to take part?
column2: 嗨。我在為《戰士週報》做問卷調查。你想參加嗎?

column1: Hobbitses? What the hell are they?
column2: 哈比人們? 那到底是什麼東西?

column1: How are you planning on getting into the Tower?
How can I prove I'm innocent?
column2: 我怎麼證明我是無辜的?

column1: How can I prove I'm not a demon?
column2: 我怎麼證明我不是惡魔?

column1: How can a tree be a problem?
How come a tree has speech apparatus?
column2: 一棵樹怎麼會有發聲器官?

column1: How come you can sell priceless antiques for so little?
column2: 你怎麼能以這麼低的價錢賣無價的古董?

column1: How come you woodworm are talking anyway?
column2: 話說回來,你們蛀蟲怎麼會說話?

column1: How come you're not talking olde worlde anymore?
column2: 你怎麼不再用古腔古調說話了?

column1: How come you've got a fruit machine in here then?
column2: 那你這裡怎麼會有吃角子老虎?

column1: How did I know this was coming...
How did you know my name?
How do you survive with such a near fatal wound?
column2: 你受了這麼近乎致命的傷怎麼還活著?

column1: How does he intend to do that?
How is Sordid helping to boost business?
column2: Sordid 怎麼幫忙促進生意?

column1: How long haven't you been playing it for?
How would you like your goat?
column2: 你的山羊肉要怎麼處理?
嗨,小子們。有什麼新鮮事?

column1: I am your Prince Charming - come to sweep you up in my arms.
column2: 我是你的白馬王子——來把妳擁入懷中。

column1: I bet they don't use this for drying clothes.
column2: 我敢打賭他們不會用這個來晾衣服。

column1: I bet you feel a proper fool.
column2: 我敢打賭你覺得自己像個十足的傻瓜。

column1: I can see my reflection in it.
I can see the dragon's treasure down there.
column2: 我能看到下面有龍的寶藏。

column1: I can see your magical auras, man.
column2: 我看得到你的魔力光環,老兄。

column1: I can turn people into stone with this.
column2: 我可以用這個把人變成石頭。

column1: I can't eat that. I'll die.

column2: 我幫不上忙——我對小孩有恐懼症。

column1: I can't imagine what use I'll have for it.
column2: 我想不出這東西能有什麼用處。

column1: I can't see any way of getting a full moon in here.
column2: 我想不出辦法把滿月弄進這裡。

column1: I can't see much down there but it smells really awful.
column2: 下面看不到什麼,但氣味真的很糟。

column1: I could make it worth your while.
I didn't expect talking woodworm if that's what you mean.
column2: 如果你的意思是這個,我可沒料到會遇到會說話的蛀蟲。

column1: I don't have that kinda money.
I don't know what you mean.
I don't like the sound of this witch person.
column2: 我不喜歡這個女巫聽起來的樣子。

column1: I don't need help from an overrated hippy!
column2: 我不需要一個被高估的嬉皮來幫忙!

column1: I don't particularly want to see anywhere.
column2: 我沒特別想去什麼地方。

column1: I don't really care for the forgiveness of a woodworm.
column2: 我不太在乎蛀蟲的原諒。

column1: I don't take orders from common farmyard animals.
column2: 我不接受普通農場動物的命令。

column1: I don't think a man on a rack is in any position to make threats.
column2: 我不認為被綁在刑架上的人有資格威脅別人。

column1: I don't think we see eye to eye on this, pal.
column2: 我不覺得我們在這件事上達成共識了,老兄。

column1: I dunno. Got any bright ideas?
column2: 我不知道。有什麼好主意嗎?

column1: I dunno. I hoped a relevant question would appear.
column2: 我不知道。我期望會出現一個相關的問題。

column1: I dunno. I'm just obeying orders.
column2: 我不知道。我只是奉命行事。

column1: I found some but it's still buried in the mountains.
column2: 我找到一些,但還埋在山裡。

column1: I got them from the oaf in the forest.
column2: 我從森林裡那個呆子那裡拿到的。

column1: I had no idea this place was a tourist attraction.
column2: 我不知道這地方是個旅遊景點。

column1: I have a fear of spiral staircases.
I have some fine examples of it in my pocket.
column2: 我口袋裡有些上等的樣品。

column1: I have this strange desire to stay alive.
column2: 我有個奇怪的念頭,就是想活著。

column1: I have to stall you until the rescue posse come along.
column2: 我得拖住你,直到救援隊趕來。

column1: I having second thoughts about this whole idea. Farewell.
column2: 我對這整個主意重新考慮了。告辭。

column1: I hope it's not like the one up the stairs...
column2: 希望不要跟樓上那個一樣……

column1: I hope this isn't an infringement of copyright.
column2: 我希望這沒有侵犯版權。

column1: I hope your bad luck's not catching.
column2: 希望你的壞運氣不會傳染。

column1: I just happen to have one right here.
I just happen to have some right here.
I just wanted to ask you some questions.
column2: 我只是想問你一些問題。

column1: I know a bargain when I see one. I'll take it.
column2: 我懂什麼是划算的。我要了。
我剛才無意間聽到你們的對話。

column1: I pounded it out of some seeds.
column2: 我從一些種子裡搗出來的。

column1: I pride myself on not talking to tree stumps OR woodworm.
column2: 我以不跟樹樁「或」蠹蟲說話為榮。

column1: I quite fancy a drink now. Make mine a wet wizard.
column2: 我現在想來一杯。給我一杯「濕潤巫師」。

column1: I really NEED to know your true names.
column2: 我真的「需要」知道你們的本名。

column1: I really think I'm wasting my time here.
column2: 我真的覺得我在這裡浪費時間。

column1: I refuse utterly to acknowledge that I am talking to a tree stump!
column2: 我斷然拒絕承認我在跟一個樹樁說話!

column1: I shall not be stopped by a lump of water and a carrot!
